rationale: sqlite3SelectNew at 1374 returns pSel. If pSel==0 (allocation failed), nothing to free. If pSel!=0, it is deleted via sqlite3SelectDelete(db,pSel) at line 1398 on that path. pSrc ownership is also handled: nulled into pSel->pSrc then deleted with the Select, or freed via sqlite3SrcListDelete at 1441. All feasible paths release the resource.

sqlite3SelectNew at 1374 returns pSel which is freed via sqlite3SelectDelete at line 1398 on the success branch; on the NULL/NOMEM branch (1377) ownership of pSrc/pExprList is reset and the function continues. pSrc is freed at 1441 via sqlite3SrcListDelete. All feasible paths release the allocated resources, so this is not a leak.

sqlite3Realloc result is assigned to pCur->aOverflow (cursor-owned buffer) when non-null; on failure (aNew==0) the original pCur->aOverflow is preserved and SQLITE_NOMEM_BKPT returned, so no leak. Ownership is transferred to the cursor structure and freed during cursor teardown, not at this call site.

sqlite3Realloc result is checked for NULL (line 5210) and on failure returns SQLITE_NOMEM_BKPT; on success it is stored in pCur->aOverflow (line 5213), which is owned by the cursor and freed during cursor teardown. No leak, no null deref, ownership is retained in the cursor structure.

In sqlite3 build.c create-index logic, pIndex from sqlite3AllocateIndexObject is either attached to the schema (ownership transferred) or freed via the exit_create_index cleanup label that the function jumps to on failure (e.g., the mallocFailed goto at line 4188). This is a standard SQLite allocate-then-goto-cleanup pattern, so it is not a real never-free leak.

sqlite3AllocateIndexObject result is stored in pIndex within sqlite3CreateIndex. On mallocFailed the code jumps to the exit_create_index label, which in SQLite frees pIndex (sqlite3DbFree) and other locals. This is the standard centralized-cleanup pattern; the allocation is owned and released on all paths via the goto-label, so it is not a true NeverFree.
The Select object allocated by sqlite3SelectNew at line 165 is freed by sqlite3SelectDelete at line 169 on the same straight-line path with no intervening early returns, so the resource is released.

pSel allocated by sqlite3SelectNew at line 165 is released by sqlite3SelectDelete(db, pSel) at line 169 on the only path, and sqlite3SelectDelete safely handles a NULL pSel. No leak or null-deref on the feasible path.

sqlite3ArrayAllocate's result is stored in pInfo->aCol, a member of the AggInfo struct passed in. Ownership is transferred to the caller's structure and freed elsewhere (during AggInfo teardown), so this is not a leak within this function.

sqlite3ArrayAllocate result is stored in pInfo->aFunc, a struct member. Ownership is transferred to the AggInfo structure, which is freed elsewhere (agginfoFree/sqlite3DbFree during cleanup). The allocation is not lost on return; the index is returned and the pointer persists in the struct.

sqlite3ArrayAllocate reallocates and the result is stored into pInfo->aCol, a member of the AggInfo structure. Ownership is transferred to the caller's structure and freed when the AggInfo is torn down, so this is not a leak at this site.

The slice shows intentional ownership transfer, not a leak, and the checker ignores SQLite's centralized cleanup.

sqlite3SelectNew() in SQLite takes ownership of the passed sub-expressions (pSrc, pWhere, the new ExprList) and frees them on its own allocation failure. The returned pSelect is itself owned by the parse context and freed via the normal SQLite parser/db cleanup (sqlite3ParserReset / error unwind), not at this call site. The slice shows ownership transfer rather than a leak, and SQLite's arena-style memory management releases these objects on the parse error path.

In SQLite's printf logic, zExtra holds the temporary buffer and is freed at the end of the formatting loop/function via sqlite3_free(zExtra). The null check at line 1016 returns early before any further use, and the standard SQLite pattern frees zExtra after the value is appended to the accumulator. The slice does not show the cleanup site, but this is the well-established sqlite3VXPrintf cleanup convention.

The buffer from sqlite3Realloc at line 1219 is stored into p->zText (line 1224) and the SQLITE_PRINTF_MALLOCED flag is set (line 1226), transferring ownership to the StrAccum struct. It is freed later via sqlite3_str_reset (used at lines 1210/1228). On the NULL path it is reset and an error is set. This is intentional ownership transfer, not a leak.

The KeyInfo allocated by sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList at line 815 is stored into the VDBE opcode's p4.pKeyInfo field. Ownership is transferred to the Vdbe structure, which releases all P4_KEYINFO resources when the prepared statement is finalized/freed. This is intentional ownership transfer, not a leak.

The KeyInfo allocated at line 6707 is handed to sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with the P4_KEYINFO operand type at line 6711, which transfers ownership to the VDBE. SQLite frees P4_KEYINFO operands when the prepared statement is finalized, so this is not a leak. Same pattern as the earlier P4_KEYINFO use at 6680-6681.

sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList allocates a KeyInfo whose ownership is transferred to the VDBE via sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with the P4_KEYINFO type. The VDBE takes responsibility for freeing the KeyInfo when the statement is finalized, so it is not leaked on this path.

sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList allocates a KeyInfo that is handed to sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with the P4_KEYINFO type. In SQLite, P4_KEYINFO transfers ownership to the VDBE, which releases the KeyInfo when the operand/VDBE is destroyed. The allocation is not leaked; ownership is intentionally transferred.

pKeyInfo from sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList is passed to sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with the P4_KEYINFO type, which transfers ownership to the VDBE. SQLite frees P4_KEYINFO operands when the VDBE is destroyed, so this is not a leak.

rationale: The KeyInfo allocated by sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList is passed to sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with P4_KEYINFO at line 6680-6681. In SQLite, P4_KEYINFO transfers ownership of the KeyInfo to the VDBE, which releases it during VDBE finalization. Same pattern applies at line 6707/6711. Ownership is intentionally transferred, so no leak.

The KeyInfo allocated by sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList at line 815 is stored into pOp->p4.pKeyInfo, an opcode field of the VDBE. Ownership is transferred to the VDBE, which releases P4_KEYINFO resources during statement finalization/cleanup. This is not a leak within the scope of this function; the allocation is intentionally retained.

The KeyInfo allocated by sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList at line 6707 is passed to sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with the P4_KEYINFO type at line 6711-6713, which transfers ownership to the VDBE. SQLite frees P4_KEYINFO operands during VDBE cleanup, so this is intentional ownership transfer, not a leak.

pKeyInfo from sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList is handed to sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with P4_KEYINFO, transferring ownership to the VDBE which manages and frees the KeyInfo. This is the standard SQLite ownership-transfer pattern, not a leak.

The KeyInfo allocated by sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList is passed as a P4_KEYINFO operand to the VDBE op. SQLite takes ownership of P4_KEYINFO memory and frees it during VDBE cleanup (freeP4/sqlite3KeyInfoUnref), so this is intentional ownership transfer, not a leak. The checker cannot see VDBE's deferred deallocation.

pKeyInfo from sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList is passed to sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with P4_KEYINFO, which transfers ownership to the VDBE; SQLite frees it during VDBE teardown. This is the standard ownership-transfer pattern, not a leak.

rationale: The KeyInfo allocated at line 6679 is immediately passed to sqlite3VdbeAddOp4 with the P4_KEYINFO marker. The VDBE takes ownership of P4_KEYINFO operands and releases them when the prepared statement is finalized (via freeP4/sqlite3KeyInfoUnref). This is intentional ownership transfer, not a leak. The same pattern is used at line 6707/6711 for the ORDER BY KeyInfo.

The mutex allocated at line 5418 is freed on the error path (5427) along with pNew, and on success ownership is transferred to the registered VFS. All feasible paths either clean up or transfer ownership.

aOut is freed on the nOut2!=nOut branch (line 733), and on the else branch ownership is transferred to sqlite3_result_blob with sqlite3_free as the destructor (line 736). All feasible paths release the allocation.

rationale: The realloc result is stored into p->azResult (a field of the caller-owned TabResult accumulator). Ownership is transferred to the caller, which frees it via sqlite3_free_table. No leak within this callback's scope.

The realloc result is assigned to p->azResult (line 61), a field of the TabResult accumulator passed in via pArg. Ownership is retained by the caller's struct and freed elsewhere (sqlite3_free_table). On realloc failure azNew==0 and the original pointer is preserved, then it goes to malloc_failed without leaking. The reported never-free path does not account for the callback's external ownership model.

sqlite3SelectNew at line 265 takes ownership of pList, pSrc, pWhere2, pGrp, pOrderBy2, pLimit2 and the resulting pSelect is freed via sqlite3SelectDelete at line 273, which recursively frees all owned sub-objects. This is SQLite's standard ownership-transfer pattern; even on allocation failure sqlite3SelectNew frees its arguments.

pSelect allocated by sqlite3SelectNew at line 265 is freed by sqlite3SelectDelete at line 273 on all paths. sqlite3SelectNew takes ownership of pList, pSrc, pWhere2, pGrp, pOrderBy2, pLimit2, and sqlite3SelectDelete recursively frees them. SQLite's allocators are no-ops on OOM (mallocFailed flag), so this is the standard, leak-safe SQLite cleanup pattern.

The memory allocated by sqlite3Realloc at line 269 is stored in pMem->zMalloc (and pMem->z), which persists in the caller-owned Mem struct. Ownership is transferred to the Mem object and freed elsewhere (e.g. sqlite3DbFreeNN at line 275 or via sqlite3VdbeMemRelease). On the NOMEM path (line 270) the old pointer is freed and szMalloc is reset to 0 at line 281. No leak on any feasible path.

sqlite3Realloc result at line 269 is stored in pMem->zMalloc and pMem->z, retaining ownership in the Mem struct. The NULL case frees the old pointer (line 270), and on failure pMem->szMalloc/z are reset (lines 278-282). The buffer is owned by pMem and freed later via normal Mem teardown, so this is not a leak.

sqlite3Realloc result is stored into the persistent struct field p->aAlloc (line 550), transferring ownership to the PmaReader object. This buffer is reused across calls and freed centrally when the PmaReader is cleared/destroyed, not within this function. No leak on the local path.

sqlite3Realloc result is stored in the persistent struct member p->aAlloc, which is owned by the PmaReader and freed during its destruction elsewhere. On allocation failure aNew is NULL, the original p->aAlloc is preserved (not overwritten/leaked), and the function returns SQLITE_NOMEM_BKPT. Ownership is retained by the long-lived PmaReader object, so this is not a never-free leak.

The reallocated buffer is stored into the persistent struct field pWal->apWiData (line 774), transferring ownership to the Wal object rather than leaking. On allocation failure apNew is NULL and the original pWal->apWiData is preserved, so no leak or double-free occurs in this slice.

sqlite3Realloc result is checked for NULL (returns SQLITE_NOMEM_BKPT on failure) and on success stored into pWal->apWiData, transferring ownership to the Wal struct which frees it elsewhere. No leak or null-deref on the shown path.

The allocated index object is immediately stored into pLoop->u.btree.pIndex (line 1117), transferring ownership to the WhereLoop structure, which is responsible for freeing it later. The allocation is not leaked but retained via a persistent data structure.

The allocated index object is immediately checked for NULL (line 1116 jumps to cleanup on failure) and on success ownership is transferred to pLoop->u.btree.pIndex (line 1117). The structure retains the pointer for later freeing/use, so this is not a never-free leak nor an unchecked null deref.

pLeft from sqlite3ExprForVectorField is passed to sqlite3PExpr (line 1481), which takes ownership of its left/right operand expressions and frees them on failure or attaches them to the returned tree. Ownership is transferred, not leaked.

pRight (and pLeft) allocated by sqlite3ExprForVectorField are passed to sqlite3PExpr, which takes ownership of the subexpressions and frees them on allocation failure. The resulting pNew is inserted into the WHERE clause with TERM_DYNAMIC so it is freed during clause teardown. This is intentional ownership transfer, not a leak.

pLeft from sqlite3ExprForVectorField is passed to sqlite3PExpr, which in SQLite takes ownership of its operand subexpressions and frees them (including on OOM). The resulting pNew is inserted into pWC with TERM_DYNAMIC, so the WhereClause owns and later frees it. This is intentional ownership transfer, not a leak.

pRight (and pLeft) from sqlite3ExprForVectorField are passed to sqlite3PExpr, which takes ownership and frees subexpressions on its own error paths. The resulting pNew is inserted into pWC via whereClauseInsert with TERM_DYNAMIC, so the WHERE clause owns and frees it during cleanup. Ownership is transferred, not leaked.

The KeyInfo allocated at line 2065 is passed to sqlite3VdbeAppendP4 with P4_KEYINFO at line 2067, transferring ownership to the VDBE. SQLite frees P4_KEYINFO-tagged P4 operands when the prepared statement/VDBE is finalized, so this is intentional ownership transfer, not a leak.

sqlite3_value_dup result is stored in p->pValue inside the aggregate context returned by sqlite3_aggregate_context. Ownership is transferred to the persistent context and is released later in the corresponding finalize/value/invert functions (e.g. first_valueFinalizeFunc), which are not part of this slice. The allocation is not leaked on the reported path.

sqlite3_value_dup result is stored in p->pValue where p is the persistent aggregate context obtained via sqlite3_aggregate_context. SQLite's window/aggregate framework frees this value in the corresponding finalize/value function (nth_valueFinalizeFunc), so ownership is retained in the context rather than leaked. The analyzer does not see the finalize callback.

p->pVal lives in the SQLite aggregate context (sqlite3_aggregate_context). The dup'd value is freed on each step (line 497) before re-assignment and is released in the corresponding finalize/value/inverse callbacks of the last_value window function. Ownership is held by the aggregate context, not leaked on this path.

sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList allocates a KeyInfo that is handed to sqlite3VdbeAppendP4 with P4_KEYINFO (line 1879), which transfers ownership to the VDBE; the VDBE frees P4_KEYINFO objects on cleanup. pKeyInfo is only non-null when this path runs, so ownership is intentionally transferred rather than leaked.

pKeyInfo from sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList is handed to the VDBE via sqlite3VdbeAppendP4 with P4_KEYINFO, transferring ownership; the VDBE frees it on destruction. This is the standard SQLite ownership-transfer pattern, not a leak.

sqlite3_value_dup result is stored in p->pValue within the aggregate context, which persists across the window function and is freed in the corresponding finalize/value/inverse callbacks (sqlite3_value_free). Ownership is intentionally retained in persistent state, so this is not a leak in the shown slice.

sqlite3_value_dup result is stored in the aggregate context field p->pValue, not lost. Ownership is transferred to the persistent NthValueCtx structure and freed later in first_valueFinalizeFunc (via sqlite3_value_free). The allocation is not leaked; the analyzer simply doesn't track the cross-function free in the aggregate finalize callback.

sqlite3_value_dup result is stored into p->pVal where p is an aggregate context owned by SQLite. The duplicated value is freed by sqlite3_value_free in the inverse/finalize callbacks (and at line 497 before reassignment), so ownership is retained in persistent context and released on later step/finalize. No leak on the shown path.

pKeyInfo from sqlite3KeyInfoFromExprList is consumed by sqlite3VdbeAppendP4 with P4_KEYINFO, which transfers ownership to the VDBE; the VDBE refcounts and frees KeyInfo objects on cleanup. If allocation fails (returns 0), the if(pKeyInfo) guard skips usage. No leak or null-deref on feasible paths.
